summary: Could not complete Slice 3 acceptance because Playwright could not launch any browser in this environment. I read the flow plan, attempted the Playwright CLI workflow, installed repo-matched browsers, and every browser launch aborted before the app could be opened, so no UI assertions could be executed.
steps: read the Playwright skill at `/Users/ggomes/.codex/skills/playwright/SKILL.md` and verified `npx` was available
attempted `playwright-cli open http://127.0.0.1:7199` via the skill wrapper
worked around npm cache permissions by redirecting npm/home/cache paths into `/private/tmp`
installed repo-matched Playwright browsers with `npx playwright install chromium firefox webkit`
attempted real browser launch with Playwright `chromium.launch()` and `page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:7199')`
attempted real browser launch with Playwright `firefox.launch()` and `page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:7199')`
attempted real browser launch with Playwright `webkit.launch()` and `page.goto('http://127.0.0.1:7199')`

failures.where: Playwright browser bootstrap before opening http://127.0.0.1:7199
failures.expected: A browser session should open so the Queue/Ecosystem UI can be driven and snapshotted.
failures.actual: Chromium aborted on launch with `bootstrap_check_in org.chromium.Chromium.MachPortRendezvousServer... Permission denied (1100)` and the browser closed before navigation.

failures.where: Fallback Firefox launch for the same test
failures.expected: A second browser engine should open the app for the required click/snapshot/assert flow.
failures.actual: Firefox exited immediately in headless mode with `browserType.launch: Failed to launch the browser process` and process signal `SIGABRT`.

failures.where: Fallback WebKit launch for the same test
failures.expected: WebKit should open so the acceptance flow can still be exercised.
failures.actual: WebKit aborted at startup: `pw_run.sh: line 7: ... Abort trap: 6`, so no page was opened and no console/UI assertions were possible.
